title: Basic Intergluteal Numismatics

jokes.text: Bro, free crackers.
jokes.setup: Serious musical performance of Radiohead's 'Creep' at a school event
jokes.punchline: Student completely ignores the emotional performance to focus on free crackers
jokes.explanation: Nice juxtaposition of high art vs. low priorities, economical delivery

dialogue.speaker: Student
dialogue.line: Those are some really good crackers.

jokes.text: Et tu, pencil?
jokes.setup: Student dealing with everyday frustrations while dramatic music plays
jokes.punchline: Classical 'Et tu, Brute?' reference applied to a betraying pencil
jokes.explanation: Clever literary reference applied to mundane object, very economical

dialogue.speaker: Student
dialogue.line: Et tu, Brute? You were supposed to be there for me!

dialogue.speaker: Student
dialogue.line: We've been through so much together, and you just... break on me now?

jokes.text: When it rains, it pours.
jokes.setup: Continuation of student's minor frustrations
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic cliché applied to trivial problems
jokes.explanation: Predictable escalation but fits the pattern of overreaction

dialogue.line: Overly dramatic cliché applied to trivial problems

jokes.text: Ass Crack Bandit!
jokes.setup: Dramatic buildup with serious music and everyday problems
jokes.punchline: Reveal that all this drama leads to the 'Ass Crack Bandit'
jokes.explanation: Perfect cold open structure - builds drama for absurd reveal, instantly memorable

dialogue.speaker: Jeff Winger
dialogue.line: We face challenges every day. Financial struggles, academic pressures, questions about our futures...

dialogue.speaker: Britta Perry
dialogue.line: We wonder if we're making the right choices.

dialogue.speaker: Troy Barnes
dialogue.line: If we're becoming the people we want to be.

dialogue.speaker: Abed Nadir
dialogue.line: But there's one threat that supersedes all of these concerns.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: The Ass Crack Bandit.

jokes.text: upper buttock fold
jokes.setup: Dean trying to address a serious security issue
jokes.punchline: Overly clinical euphemism for 'ass crack'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Dean's awkwardness with direct language

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: We need to address the gluteal cleavage situation immediately.

jokes.text: when did we split the gazette from the mirror?
jokes.setup: Student reporters from different publications asking questions
jokes.punchline: Dean's confusion about having multiple school newspapers
jokes.explanation: Good world-building joke about Greendale's bureaucratic confusion

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: Wait, we have more than one newspaper?

dialogue.speaker: Student Reporter 1
dialogue.line: The Greendale Gazette, sir.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: Right, right.

dialogue.speaker: Student Reporter 2
dialogue.line: And The Community College Times.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: Oh my god, how long has this been happening?

jokes.text: Will this affect soccer?
jokes.setup: Serious press conference about campus security
jokes.punchline: Reporter asks completely unrelated question about soccer
jokes.explanation: Perfect non sequitur showing how different priorities clash

dialogue.speaker: Reporter
dialogue.line: What's your stance on the new soccer field proposal?

dialogue.speaker: Raul Lopez
dialogue.line: I... we're in the middle of a campus security briefing.

jokes.text: Hanes his ways.
jokes.setup: Dean revealing details about the bandit's letter
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'Hanes' underwear brand and 'hence his ways'
jokes.explanation: Standard pun, not particularly clever but fits Dean's character

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: The bandit's letter says he robs banks to buy Hanes underwear.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: Hence his ways.

jokes.text: He should be called 'the run-on sentence bandit.'
jokes.setup: Reading the bandit's rambling, poorly written letter
jokes.punchline: Annie's precise observation about the writing quality
jokes.explanation: Perfect Annie moment - grammatically precise and character-specific

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: This is barely literate. The formatting is atrocious. And he uses 'your' instead of 'you're.' Twice.

jokes.text: if that's the highest DEFCON, and if high DEFCONs are worse than low ones
jokes.setup: Dean trying to sound authoritative about security
jokes.punchline: Immediately undermines himself by admitting ignorance
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Dean's insecurity masked as authority

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: I want to assure you that security at Greendale is my top priority. We have state-of-the-art protocols in place.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: Well, actually, I have no idea what those protocols are. I just nod when the security guy talks to me.

jokes.text: She's still alive, but I've put in my time.
jokes.setup: Duncan explaining his absence caring for sick mother
jokes.punchline: Callous admission he's done his duty regardless of outcome
jokes.explanation: Dark but perfectly crafted character moment, economical and memorable

dialogue.speaker: Duncan
dialogue.line: My mother's been ill. I've been taking care of her.

dialogue.speaker: Duncan
dialogue.line: But I've done my duty. Whether she makes it or not, that's on her now.

jokes.text: Oh, Pierce, good for you. I always thought that hairpiece was a bit cowardly.
jokes.setup: Duncan returns from caring for his mother
jokes.punchline: Britta mistakes his absence for cosmetic surgery
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Britta's tendency to misread situations

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Duncan, what did you have done? You look amazing!

dialogue.speaker: Duncan
dialogue.line: I was caring for my mother.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Oh my God, you went to Switzerland!

jokes.text: since we held that rally protesting the wrong Korea
jokes.setup: Dean discussing PR crises at Greendale
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of hilariously incompetent past mistake
jokes.explanation: Perfect Greendale joke - absurd institutional incompetence delivered casually

dialogue.speaker: Dean
dialogue.line: We've had our share of PR crises at Greendale. Remember when I accidentally approved the budget to build a statue of myself made entirely of marzipan? In July? In Arizona? The statue lasted three hours before it became a puddle of Dean-flavored disappointment.

jokes.text: Double fruit bonus. Crikey! Kiwi combo, mate. You can't handle the fruit.
jokes.setup: Serious investigation scene
jokes.punchline: Abed playing fruit-themed video game with over-the-top commentary
jokes.explanation: Classic Abed obliviousness to serious situations around him

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: This is serious. We need to investigate who ate the last bagel. I'm going to document everything.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Okay, I'm playing Fruit Ninja now. HYAH! SLICE! The watermelon is destroyed! Another combo! Oh no, the banana got away! This is the most intense moment of my life!

jokes.text: No! Unfair and racist.
jokes.setup: Chang apparently losing at the fruit game
jokes.punchline: Typical Chang overreaction calling game mechanics racist
jokes.explanation: Predictable Chang moment but perfectly in character

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: This game is racist! The fruit is clearly biased against me!

jokes.text: I'd rather just stop at the meat store.
jokes.setup: Troy leaving voicemail about dinner options
jokes.punchline: Calling a butcher shop 'the meat store'
jokes.explanation: Good Troy childlike language pattern - overly simple but endearing

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Hey, uh, I was thinking we could grab dinner tonight. Maybe hit up that new place downtown? Or we could go to the meat store if you're in the mood for, uh, steaks or whatever. The meat store. Yeah.

jokes.text: This is how they comfort victims in movies.
jokes.setup: Someone trying to comfort Troy with blankets after being cracked
jokes.punchline: Troy's meta observation about movie comfort tropes
jokes.explanation: Nice meta moment but not particularly distinctive

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: You know what? In every movie, when someone's sad, they just throw a blanket over them and suddenly everything's better. But here's the thing — I'm still sad. The blanket didn't work. I think movies have been lying to us this whole time.

jokes.text: Well, guess what, your two cents is change, and it's banned.
jokes.setup: Dean banning change from campus, someone objects
jokes.punchline: Dean's literal interpretation of 'two cents' idiom
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay showing Dean's absurd logic taken to extremes

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: Effective immediately, I am banning all change from Greendale Community College campus.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Dean, that's insane. What about people who need to do laundry?

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: That's their problem. I want to hear what people think, and if they want to give me their two cents, they're going to have to give me actual pennies and nickels.

jokes.text: You can't stop me, because what are you gonna do, not have butts?
jokes.setup: Bandit's threatening letter
jokes.punchline: Absurd but logically sound reasoning
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd villain logic that's somehow undeniable

dialogue.speaker: Ass Crack Bandit
dialogue.line: I know what you're thinking. How can someone with such a narrow entrance get in and out so quickly? The answer is simple: practice, dedication, and a strictly regimented diet of fiber supplements and prune juice.

jokes.text: Yeah, and I have evidence that shows I'm not listening. Exhibit 'A,' exhibit 'B.' Lalalalalala.
jokes.setup: Annie trying to present evidence to the Dean
jokes.punchline: Dean's completely childish response with fake legal language
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dean moment - authority figure acting like a child

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Dean, I have evidence that proves—

dialogue.speaker: Dean
dialogue.line: I'm going to stop you right there. According to subsection 12, paragraph B of the imaginary bylaws I just made up, anything you say is stupid and also you're stupid.

jokes.text: Taking it easy is how Troy ended up with a quarter of a buck in his crack.
jokes.setup: Jeff telling Annie to take it easy
jokes.punchline: Annie's pun on 'quarter' and 'quarter of a buck'
jokes.explanation: Functional pun but not particularly clever

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Just take it easy.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Why? A quarter of a buck is still a quarter!

jokes.text: The important question is 'who cares?'
jokes.setup: Deep discussion about the bandit's psychology
jokes.punchline: Jeff's typical dismissive attitude
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff deflection but not particularly original

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Look, I'm not a psychologist. The guy steals things and wears a mask. That's literally it. Stop trying to make it deep.

jokes.text: Okay, squeal.
jokes.setup: Jeff just said 'no squealing' about their investigation
jokes.punchline: Annie immediately does the opposite
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate callback with great economy and character consistency

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: No squealing? But that's my favorite part!

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: *squeals excitedly*

jokes.text: Safety pants, 100 bucks a pop.
jokes.setup: Campus dealing with bandit crisis
jokes.punchline: Overpriced 'safety pants' being sold
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist capitalism response to crisis. Shows opportunistic mindset.

dialogue.speaker: Vendor
dialogue.line: Safety pants! Get your safety pants here! Protect yourself from bandits with our reinforced denim!

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: How much are those?

dialogue.speaker: Vendor
dialogue.line: Forty dollars a pair.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Forty dollars? For pants?

dialogue.speaker: Vendor
dialogue.line: Safety pants. They're different.

jokes.text: ♪ It said quarter to five, but it was quarter to ass ♪
jokes.setup: Song about the Ass Crack Bandit on the radio
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'quarter to five' with 'quarter to ass'
jokes.explanation: Clever song parody with good wordplay structure

dialogue.speaker: Radio Singer
dialogue.line: It's a quarter to ass.

jokes.text: We know that he hates money or loves it or doesn't care about money and hates butts or loves them.
jokes.setup: Duncan trying to profile the bandit
jokes.punchline: Completely unhelpful analysis that covers all possibilities
jokes.explanation: Good satire of useless psychological profiling

dialogue.speaker: Duncan
dialogue.line: Based on the evidence, the bandit is either male or female, likely between the ages of 5 and 85. They probably have a face. They may or may not be wearing clothes. The bandit could be anyone in this room... or no one. They might strike again... or they might not.

jokes.text: Using a social disorder as a procedural device. Mildly autistic super detectives everywhere.
jokes.setup: Someone asking Abed to use his special abilities
jokes.punchline: Abed's meta critique of TV tropes including his own character
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta moment where character critiques his own TV trope usage

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: You want me to use my powers? Fine. Here's what's going to happen: I'll make a reference to a TV show, everyone will laugh because they got it, and we'll all feel smart. Then I'll point out that my character exists only to make references, which is itself a reference to postmodern television. Then someone will say 'that's so Abed,' and we'll have achieved meta-comedy inception. But here's the thing—by acknowledging the trope, I'm not transcending it. I'm just another character in a show about characters who are aware they're in a show. We're all just doing what the writers want us to do. Even this speech. Especially this speech.

jokes.text: It hurts.
jokes.setup: Abed continuing his critique of bad TV writing
jokes.punchline: Physical pain from encountering terrible tropes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed beat - ultra-economical expression of his critical nature

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: The problem with bad TV writing is it's not just intellectually offensive, it's physically painful. Like, my brain is literally rejecting it. I can feel synapses misfiring. This is what it must be like to be allergic to incompetence.

jokes.text: Then I think you just admitted you already owned a fake butt.
jokes.setup: Chang saying the decoy butt was free
jokes.punchline: Hickey's logical deduction about Chang's possessions
jokes.explanation: Perfect logical trap with uncomfortable implications for Chang

dialogue.speaker: Hickey
dialogue.line: If the decoy butt was free, then everything Chang owns is stolen.

jokes.text: I mean, why mix metaphors? Ants don't have butts.
jokes.setup: Reading another rambling bandit letter
jokes.punchline: Annie's precise grammatical critique of mixed metaphors
jokes.explanation: Great Annie moment focusing on technical writing issues over the crime

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Dear Greendale, you've been a real thorn in my side, but also a refreshing breeze that's been breathing down my neck.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Mixed metaphor. A thorn and a breeze are two completely different things. You can't be both a thorn in someone's side AND a breeze breathing down their neck. It's grammatically inconsistent and logically incoherent.

jokes.text: Ants do have butts.
jokes.setup: Annie claiming ants don't have butts
jokes.punchline: Hickey's matter-of-fact biological correction
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan correction that derails Annie's critique

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Ants don't have butts.

dialogue.speaker: Hickey
dialogue.line: Yes they do. They have an abdomen.

jokes.text: Oh, excuse me for being alive in the '90s and having two ears connected to a heart.
jokes.setup: Being mocked for being a Dave Matthews fan
jokes.punchline: Jeff's over-dramatic defense of his musical taste
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff vanity moment with melodramatic phrasing

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Hey, Dave Matthews is a musical genius! The man plays violin AND sings. Do you know how hard that is? He's like if Mozart and a really attractive guy had a baby.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: And 'Crash Into Me'? That's not just a song, that's a transcendent experience. It's what plays in God's car when he's driving to work.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: You people wouldn't know artistry if it serenaded you with a jam band and made you feel feelings you didn't know you could feel.

jokes.text: Done.
jokes.setup: Need to assemble list of Dave Matthews fans among faculty
jokes.punchline: Jeff immediately finishes because he's apparently the only one
jokes.explanation: Perfect punchline with maximum economy - implies Jeff is the only DMB fan

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Okay, so who here is a Dave Matthews fan? Anyone? Anyone at all?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Well, I guess that's the list.

jokes.text: Is better than a quarter in your butt.
jokes.setup: Discussing bathroom cameras as security measure
jokes.punchline: Troy's matter-of-fact assessment of privacy vs. assault
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective logic from Troy's perspective

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: I'm not comfortable with cameras in the bathroom.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: But I'm also not comfortable with getting assaulted in the bathroom.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: So I guess it's a wash.

jokes.text: Bend over with friends over.
jokes.setup: Dean explaining safety in numbers concept
jokes.punchline: Awkward rhyming safety slogan
jokes.explanation: Typical Dean awkward slogan attempt

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: Safety in numbers, people. That's why I always travel in groups of at least three.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: One to keep watch, one to keep... a... notch, and one to... spots... the... crotch!

jokes.text: The student that attended can vouch for him.
jokes.setup: Explaining teacher's alibi at science dance
jokes.punchline: Implication only one student attended
jokes.explanation: Subtle joke about Greendale's unpopular events delivered matter-of-factly

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: The teacher said he was at the science dance all night.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Wait, if he was at the science dance all night, how come only one student showed up?

jokes.text: This extra long churro tastes good in my real mouth.
jokes.setup: Chang eating while they investigate
jokes.punchline: Unnecessary specification that he's using his 'real' mouth
jokes.explanation: Classic Chang non-sequitur with absurd specificity

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: What? I'm eating with my real mouth.

jokes.text: It's a mild, kind sativa, perfect for playing guitar.
jokes.setup: Teacher being caught/arrested
jokes.punchline: Completely unprompted detailed weed review
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing misplaced priorities

dialogue.speaker: Professor Bublitz
dialogue.line: This stuff is incredible. I mean, the flavor profile is complex — you're getting notes of citrus, maybe some pine. The high is very cerebral, very clean. No paranoia, no couch lock. And the ash? Silver. Silver ash, man.

jokes.text: Don't tell me what I can't do. What do you think you are, Cosmo's July quiz?
jokes.setup: Annie saying Dean can't suspend her
jokes.punchline: Bizarre comparison to a magazine quiz
jokes.explanation: Completely unexpected and specific pop culture reference as insult

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: You can't suspend me.

dialogue.speaker: Dean
dialogue.line: Oh, I can't? Watch me. It's like those magazines quizzes: 'Which Douchebag Are You?' I'm all of them.

jokes.text: I think you two like to partner up on cutesy capers so you can hold hands in the dark and address your urges in semi-acceptable scenarios.
jokes.setup: Dean confronting Jeff and Annie about their partnership
jokes.punchline: Overly specific psychoanalysis of their relationship dynamic
jokes.explanation: Sharp character insight delivered with Dean's particular phrasing

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: I've been observing you two, and what I'm seeing is a classic case of intellectual codependency masked as academic collaboration. Jeff, you're projecting your father issues onto Annie's need for validation, while Annie, you're unconsciously seeking a father figure through Jeff's superficial charm and emotional unavailability.

jokes.text: Every morning, in the water... Mm.
jokes.setup: Dean assigning Jeff to coach water polo as punishment
jokes.punchline: Dean's suggestive satisfaction with the image
jokes.explanation: Classic Dean moment showing his attraction to Jeff

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: Jeff, you're going to coach our water polo team as punishment.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff Winger
dialogue.line: Water polo? Dean, I don't know anything about water polo.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: Well, you're going to learn. And you're going to look *very* good in those tight coaching shorts.

jokes.text: We're friends!
jokes.setup: Bandit suggesting Jeff and Annie have romantic tension
jokes.punchline: Their immediate defensive synchronized response
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat showing their obvious discomfort with the suggestion

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: We don't have romantic tension.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Yeah, there's nothing there.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Absolutely nothing.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Zero tension.

jokes.text: Oh, that's very interesting. I myself was in 4h.
jokes.setup: Bandit making threatening statements
jokes.punchline: Dean's completely irrelevant personal sharing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dean moment - inappropriate sharing during crisis

dialogue.speaker: Bandit
dialogue.line: You better watch your back, Dean. I know where you live.

dialogue.speaker: Dean
dialogue.line: Well, I appreciate the concern, but actually I've been meaning to tell someone—I've been going to a yoga class on Tuesday nights, and I think it's really changing my core strength. The instructor says I have natural flexibility for someone my age.

jokes.text: We have stables?
jokes.setup: Discovering the call came from the stables
jokes.punchline: Annie's surprise that Greendale has stables
jokes.explanation: Good world-building joke about Greendale's random facilities

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Greendale has stables?

jokes.text: Add some doilies and a foot bath, and this is my mom's house.
jokes.setup: Commenting on the creepy stable environment
jokes.punchline: Jeff's specific comparison to his mother's decorating
jokes.explanation: Good character detail about Jeff's background

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: This place has the exact same creepy stable environment as my mother's house. Same lighting, same furniture, same smell of regret and horse feed.

jokes.text: Since faking his death to escape meth charges, Alex 'Starburns' Osbourne has been living in the stables, eating garbage, and trying to build a cat car.
jokes.setup: Finding Starburns in the stables
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd list of his activities
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with callback to previous storylines

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: What are you doing out here?

dialogue.speaker: Starburns
dialogue.line: I'm training horses to do my taxes.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Starburns
dialogue.line: And teaching them to speak French. Also, I'm building a spaceship out of hay.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Starburns, that's insane.

dialogue.speaker: Starburns
dialogue.line: Wait until you hear about my plans to marry the head horse and open a nightclub.

jokes.text: Oh, American high five. Sorry.
jokes.setup: Failed high five attempt
jokes.punchline: Chang's explanation implying different international high five styles
jokes.explanation: Good Chang moment with his typical cultural confusion

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: In Taiwan, we do it like this. Different countries, different high fives.

jokes.text: Or maybe the Dean was right about us.
jokes.setup: Annie pointing out inconsistencies in the case
jokes.punchline: Jeff deflecting to the relationship subtext
jokes.explanation: Character moment showing Jeff's avoidance through deflection

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: The evidence doesn't match up. The timeline is inconsistent, and the witness statements contradict each other.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: You know what? I think what's really going on here is that you care about me. A lot.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: What? Jeff, focus—

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: No, it's okay. I see it. And honestly, I'm flattered.

jokes.text: It's platonic shoulder holding.
jokes.setup: Physical contact between Jeff and Annie
jokes.punchline: Jeff's over-specific clarification
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Jeff's discomfort with intimacy

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Jeff, why did you touch my shoulder like that?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: It was a supportive touch. Platonic. Entirely non-romantic and completely appropriate for a study group member to give to another study group member in a moment of emotional vulnerability.

jokes.text: Change is money! What are you guys, millionaires?
jokes.setup: Students throwing change around casually
jokes.punchline: Vendor's exasperation at wasting money
jokes.explanation: Basic observational humor about money waste

dialogue.speaker: Student vendor
dialogue.line: Do you have any idea how much money you're throwing around like it's nothing?

dialogue.speaker: Student vendor
dialogue.line: This is actual currency! People work for this!

jokes.text: Real fans call him Dave.
jokes.setup: Duncan revealing he's a Dave Matthews fan
jokes.punchline: Using Jeff's exact earlier phrase
jokes.explanation: Good callback that reveals Duncan as potential suspect

dialogue.speaker: Duncan
dialogue.line: I'm a huge Dave Matthews fan.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: That's streets ahead.

jokes.text: Lee Roth?
jokes.setup: Someone mentioning 'Dave'
jokes.punchline: Hickey thinking of David Lee Roth instead
jokes.explanation: Good generational gap humor showing Hickey's age

dialogue.speaker: Hickey
dialogue.line: David Lee Roth? Now that's a man who knew how to live. The guy had it all - the bandana, the splits, the screaming fans.

jokes.text: I don't call nobody don't call me.
jokes.setup: Denying making the phone call
jokes.punchline: Starburns' convoluted double-negative phrasing
jokes.explanation: Good Starburns character moment with his particular speech pattern

dialogue.speaker: Starburns
dialogue.line: I didn't not make that phone call.

jokes.text: I use an older, British form of Facebook called 'mug-scroll.'
jokes.setup: Duncan mentioning photos online
jokes.punchline: Fake British version of Facebook with literal name
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay combining British slang with social media concept

dialogue.speaker: Duncan
dialogue.line: I saw some photos of you online.

dialogue.speaker: Duncan
dialogue.line: On FaceBrit.

jokes.text: Oh, bloody hell, my shoe is untied by British standards.
jokes.setup: Duncan bending down in vulnerable position
jokes.punchline: Overly specific British qualification for shoelace status
jokes.explanation: Perfect Duncan Britishness obsession. Great setup for the attack.

dialogue.speaker: Duncan
dialogue.line: I say, these laces require a minimum of seven-and-three-quarter inches of structural integrity to maintain proper pedestrian safety standards, as per the Royal Academy of Footwear Sciences.

jokes.text: Oh, bloody hell, my shoe is untied by British standards.
jokes.setup: Duncan bending down to tie his shoe
jokes.punchline: Overly specific cultural qualifier for a universal situation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with Duncan's unnecessary cultural specificity

dialogue.speaker: Duncan
dialogue.line: I'm bending down to tie my shoe in a way that respects the indigenous footwear traditions of the Pacific Northwest.

jokes.text: Pierce is dead.
jokes.setup: Chase scene and investigation comedy
jokes.punchline: Sudden shift to serious news about character death
jokes.explanation: Perfect tonal whiplash - maximum economy for maximum impact

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: We need to find him before he gets away!

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I'm on it! Which way did he go?

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: According to my calculations, he's heading toward the library.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Then that's where we're going!

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: Excuse me, students. I have some terrible news.

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: What is it, Dean?

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: We just received word that Professor Professorson has passed away.

jokes.text: If you're listening, Pierce, you were a hell of a d&d player.
jokes.setup: Radio tribute to Pierce's death
jokes.punchline: Specific callback to D&D episodes
jokes.explanation: Nice memorial callback to previous episodes

dialogue.speaker: Real Neil
dialogue.line: This is Real Neil from the radio, paying tribute to Pierce Hawthorne. He was like the dragon from our D&D campaign—old, powerful, and ultimately defeated by his own greed and lack of armor.

jokes.text: It's a container for all this little stuff, and you get caught up in it. And then, the container just... Pbbt.
jokes.setup: Jeff reflecting on life and Pierce's death
jokes.punchline: Profound thought ending with silly sound effect
jokes.explanation: Good character moment mixing Jeff's depth with deflection

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Pierce may be gone, but he's still here in all of us. In our hearts, in our memories, in the way he taught us to live life to the fullest. And in his outstanding collection of racist memorabilia. Psssshhhhh!

jokes.text: Don't worry. I got a feeling he'll be back. Or her.
jokes.setup: Annie worried about the case going cold
jokes.punchline: Jeff's confidence followed by gender correction
jokes.explanation: Standard setup with mild misdirection for political correctness

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Jeff, I'm worried this case is going cold.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Don't worry. I've got the killer instinct of a man on a mission.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Woman.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: A woman on a mission.

jokes.text: But dogs cost money, your money. By donating to this project, you'll be like an investor, except without taking all my profits.
jokes.setup: Starburns pitching his cat car crowdfunding
jokes.punchline: Honest admission that investors get nothing
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist pitch with accidentally honest business model

dialogue.speaker: Starburns
dialogue.line: So you give me money, and in return... you get absolutely nothing. No equity, no updates, no cat car rides. Just the knowledge that you helped make a dream real.

dialogue.speaker: Investor
dialogue.line: That's... that's not how crowdfunding works.

dialogue.speaker: Starburns
dialogue.line: Isn't it though?

tmdb_overview: With the mysterious Ass Crack Bandit back at Greendale, Annie and Jeff are on the case. As clues and suspicions mount, Shirley delivers shocking news.
insight: Butt-coin heist episode mines 61 jokes in 45 minutes—absurdist comedy at peak density.
